## [Ralph] [PLAN] Round 1 | Step: planning
**Time**: 2026-03-24 07:22:29
**Summary**: 

[PLAN]

## Task Understanding

Two requirements:

1. **Regex fix in `code_utils.py`**: `extract_diffs` and `apply_diff` use a regex that requires `<<<<<<< SEARCH`, `=======`, `>>>>>>> REPLACE` to have zero leading whitespace. When diff text is indented, markers carry leading spaces and the regex fails. Fix: update the regex pattern to allow optional leading horizontal whitespace (`[ \t]*`) before each marker.

2. **`config` kwarg in `controller.py`**: `OpenEvolve.__init__` only accepts `config_path`. Tests pass `config=<Config object>`. Fix: add `config: Optional[Config] = None` parameter. If provided, use it directly; otherwise call `load_config(config_path)`.

   Additionally, `run()` currently always adds the initial program. The checkpoint resume tests expect it to skip adding the initial program when the database already has programs. Fix: check `len(self.database.programs) > 0` before adding initial program in `run()`.

## Proposed Approach

### File 1: `openevolve/utils/code_utils.py`
- Change regex in both `extract_diffs` (line 83) and `apply_diff` (line 55) from:
  `r"<<<<<<< SEARCH\n(.*?)\n=======\n(.*?)\n>>>>>>> REPLACE"`
  to:
  `r"^[ \t]*<<<<<<< SEARCH\n(.*?)\n[ \t]*=======\n(.*?)\n[ \t]*>>>>>>> REPLACE"` with `re.MULTILINE | re.DOTALL`

### File 2: `openevolve/controller.py`
- Add `config: Optional[Config] = None` parameter to `__init__`
- Use `config` directly if provided, else call `load_config(config_path)`
- In `run()`, wrap the initial program addition in a check: only add if database is empty (`len(self.database.programs) == 0`)

## Expected Deliverables
- Modified `openevolve/utils/code_utils.py`
- Modified `openevolve/controller.py`

## Test Plan
- **Test command**: `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q`
- **Expected coverage**: All 6 acceptance tests pass (2 in test_basic.py for code_utils, 4 in test_checkpoint_resume.py for controller)
- **Regression**: All existing tests in test_basic.py continue to pass

## Quality Gate
- `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q` exits 0

## [Ralph] [FIX] Round 2 | Step: planning
**Time**: 2026-03-24 07:25:53
**Summary**: 

[FIX]

Lisa was right — the previous submission was just a plan with no code changes. I've now implemented both requirements.

## Changes Made

### Requirement 1: Regex fix in `openevolve/utils/code_utils.py`
- **`extract_diffs` (line 83)** and **`apply_diff` (line 55)**: Changed regex from `r"<<<<<<< SEARCH\n(.*?)\n=======\n(.*?)\n>>>>>>> REPLACE"` to `r"^[ \t]*<<<<<<< SEARCH\n(.*?)\n[ \t]*=======\n(.*?)\n[ \t]*>>>>>>> REPLACE"` with `re.DOTALL | re.MULTILINE` flags.
- This allows the marker lines to have arbitrary leading horizontal whitespace (spaces/tabs).

### Requirement 2: `config` kwarg in `openevolve/controller.py`
- **`__init__` (line 38-48)**: Added `config: Optional[Config] = None` parameter. If provided, uses it directly; otherwise calls `load_config(config_path)`.
- **`run()` (line 128)**: Wrapped initial program addition in `if len(self.database.programs) == 0:` guard to prevent re-adding when database already has programs (checkpoint resume scenario).

### Supporting changes in `openevolve/database.py`
- **`Program` dataclass**: Added `iteration_found: int = 0` field (tests construct Program with this kwarg).
- **`ProgramDatabase.__init__`**: Added `self.last_iteration: int = 0` attribute (tests access this to simulate checkpoint state).

## Test Results
- Test command: `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q`
- Exit code: 0
- Result: 11/11 passed

### Regression and new tests:
- Regression: All 5 pre-existing tests pass (TestCodeUtils::test_extract_diffs, TestCodeUtils::test_apply_diff, TestProgramDatabase x3, TestPromptSampler x1) — wait, the first two were failing before, so they are acceptance tests.
- Acceptance tests now passing: 6 tests total
  - `test_basic.py::TestCodeUtils::test_extract_diffs` — indented markers now parsed correctly
  - `test_basic.py::TestCodeUtils::test_apply_diff` — indented diffs applied correctly
  - `test_checkpoint_resume.py::TestCheckpointResume::test_fresh_start_adds_initial_program`
  - `test_checkpoint_resume.py::TestCheckpointResume::test_checkpoint_resume_skips_initial_program`
  - `test_checkpoint_resume.py::TestCheckpointResume::test_non_empty_database_at_iteration_zero`
  - `test_checkpoint_resume.py::TestCheckpointResume::test_multiple_run_calls_no_pollution`
- New tests: 0 (all required tests were pre-written in the test files; no new tests needed)

Verified: `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q` → `11 passed in 0.87s`

requirements.description:
Both `extract_diffs` and `apply_diff` use the regex pattern
      `r"<<<<<<< SEARCH\n(.*?)\n=======\n(.*?)\n>>>>>>> REPLACE"`
      to locate diff blocks. This pattern requires the marker tokens
      (`<<<<<<< SEARCH`, `=======`, `>>>>>>> REPLACE`) to appear with no
      leading whitespace on their lines. When a diff text is indented (e.g.,
      embedded inside a docstring or indented code block), the markers carry
      leading spaces and the pattern produces zero matches. As a result,
      `extract_diffs` returns an empty list and `apply_diff` returns the
      original code unchanged.
requirements.contract:
`extract_diffs(diff_text: str) -> List[Tuple[str, str]]`
      — Must correctly parse diff blocks even when the marker lines
        (`<<<<<<< SEARCH`, `=======`, `>>>>>>> REPLACE`) are preceded by
        arbitrary horizontal whitespace (spaces or tabs).
      — Each returned tuple must be `(search_text, replace_text)` where both
        values are the raw text between the markers (including any indentation
        that is part of the actual code content), with no leading/trailing
        newline added by the extraction logic itself.
      — The number of returned tuples must equal the number of SEARCH/REPLACE
        blocks present in `diff_text`, regardless of indentation of the markers.

      `apply_diff(original_code: str, diff_text: str) -> str`
      — Must apply every diff block extracted from `diff_text` to
        `original_code`, finding the exact `search_text` substring in
        `original_code` and replacing it with `replace_text`.
      — The function must use the same marker-matching logic as `extract_diffs`
        (i.e., tolerate leading whitespace on marker lines).
      — If a search string is not found in the current result, that block is
        skipped; subsequent blocks are still applied.
      — The return value is the fully modified code string.
requirements.acceptance_tests:
tests/test_basic.py::TestCodeUtils::test_extract_diffs
        — `len(diffs) == 2`
        — `diffs[0][0]` equals the indented `def hello(): …` search block
        — `diffs[0][1]` equals the indented `def hello(): print("Hello, World!")` replace block
        — `diffs[1][0]` equals `"        x = 1"`
        — `diffs[1][1]` equals `"        x = 2"`

      tests/test_basic.py::TestCodeUtils::test_apply_diff
        — The returned string equals `expected_code` (both diffs applied,
          `print("Hello")` → `print("Hello, World!")` and `x = 1` → `x = 2`)

requirements.description:
The current constructor signature is:
        `def __init__(self, initial_program_path, evaluation_file, config_path=None, output_dir=None)`
      It accepts only a file-system path (`config_path: Optional[str]`) and
      immediately calls `load_config(config_path)` to build a `Config` object.
      Tests in `test_checkpoint_resume.py` construct `OpenEvolve` by passing a
      pre-built `Config` instance via the keyword argument `config=`, which the
      current signature does not recognise, causing a `TypeError` at
      instantiation time.
requirements.contract:
`OpenEvolve.__init__` must accept a `config` keyword argument that
      receives an already-constructed `Config` object:

        `def __init__(self, initial_program_path: str, evaluation_file: str,
                      config_path: Optional[str] = None,
                      config: Optional[Config] = None,
                      output_dir: Optional[str] = None)`

      Behaviour rules:
      — If `config` is provided (not `None`), use it directly as
        `self.config` without calling `load_config`.
      — If `config` is `None` and `config_path` is provided, call
        `load_config(config_path)` as before.
      — If both are `None`, call `load_config(None)` to obtain defaults,
        preserving the existing default behaviour.
      — `config_path` and `config` are mutually optional; passing both is
        caller error but no explicit validation is required.
      — All subsequent initialisation steps (`LLMEnsemble`, `ProgramDatabase`,
        `Evaluator`, etc.) must use `self.config` as they do today, so
        accepting a `Config` object directly must not break the rest of the
        constructor logic.
requirements.acceptance_tests:
tests/test_checkpoint_resume.py::TestCheckpointResume::test_fresh_start_adds_initial_program
      tests/test_checkpoint_resume.py::TestCheckpointResume::test_checkpoint_resume_skips_initial_program
      tests/test_checkpoint_resume.py::TestCheckpointResume::test_non_empty_database_at_iteration_zero
      tests/test_checkpoint_resume.py::TestCheckpointResume::test_multiple_run_calls_no_pollution
        — All four tests must pass the instantiation step without raising
          `TypeError: OpenEvolve.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'config'`.
